Caroline Doherty is evidence that there's no et path to a dream.

Doherty committed to Holy Cross in Septem- ber and will join up with the Crusaders in fall 2026 to play for coach Katie Lachapelle at an up- and-coming program in Hockey East. She'lljoin Spitfires teammate Violet Carroll (Williston Northampton) in Worcester. (She's still undecid- ed where she will skate next season.)

Hingham's star senior forward — committed to play at Holy Cross after a post-grad year — has done it all while playing for the town she grew up in. She's the school's all-time leading scorer, a two-time Patriot League MVP, and one of the state's most talented players.

Doherty made a switch at the club level this year, moving to the Massachusetts Spitfires — ranked as one of the top programs not just in the state, but in the country. The move was all Do- herty's doing — she reached out to coach Joe Hulbig to set up an opportunity, and even though the Spitfires had a full roster, she impressed enough to make the team. At their first tourna- ment of the year over Labor Day weekend, Hul- big said every college coach he talked to wanted to know about her.
